intro: Platantheroides magdalenensis, also known as the Magdalen's orchid, is a species of orchid native to the mountainous regions of North America. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antioxidant, which can help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and sore throats. The bioactive constituents of Platantheroides magdalenensis include iridoid glycosides, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to possess antimicrobial and antiviral properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support respiratory health and boost the immune system.
